title: Continuous positive airway pressure to reduce the risk of early peripheral oxygen desaturation following apnoea onset in children: Randomised double-blind controlled trial
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f70ro","text":"It was a randomised double-blind parallel controlled clinical trial, in a tertiary teaching hospital with 68 children, aging 2-6 years, ASA I-II, who presented for surgery under general anaesthesia, divided into two groups of 34 participants (CPAP and controls). The intervention was CPAP-ventilation and passive CPAP-oxygenation using the anaesthesia workstation. The main outcome measure was the occurrence of a SpO2 of 95% during a follow-up period of 300 seconds from the beginning of the apnoea. We alternatively registered the time from the onset of apnoea until a SpO2 of 95% (limited to 300 seconds) – with SpO2 recorded each 30 seconds - (T1) and time from a SpO2 of 95% to previous baseline levels aided by positive pressure ventilation (T2).  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":417,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":574,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":618,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":671,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":752,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ek6hb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"","data":{"amount":"","guid":"7B9D5990417911EBB266213794686A8B","id":0,"label":"","unit":1}}}}
steps: Continuous positive airway pressure to reduce the risk of early peripheral oxygen desaturation following apnoea onset in children: Randomised double-blind controlled trial
